1	"""Durable run-state checkpointer — local, data-sovereign (ADR-0003).
2	
3	The orchestration graph persists its full state (current phase, gate decisions, locked
4	hypotheses, verdicts) through a checkpointer so a run can interrupt at a human-approval gate
5	and resume later. That state holds the entire analysis, so the store is **on-box only**
6	(ADR-0003); a SaaS checkpointer is disqualified.
7	
8	This module is the LangGraph checkpointer factory, ported faithfully from the prior
9	platform's hardened implementation and generalized (dataset-agnostic ``ASSAY_*`` env, no
10	config-file coupling — see ADR-0006). The hardened behaviors are deliberately preserved
11	because each prevents a real production failure:
12	
13	- a **self-healing ConnectionPool** (not a raw connection): when Postgres goes away — node
14	  sleep, restart, idle timeout, a network blip — a cached raw connection is permanently
15	  broken and every later checkpoint op raises, i.e. durability is lost exactly during the
16	  interrupt/resume the checkpointer exists to support. The pool runs a liveness check on
17	  checkout and replaces dead connections;
18	- a **session advisory lock** serializing first-time schema setup across *processes*, run on
19	  the *same* connection as the DDL (a pool would otherwise check out a different connection,
20	  leaving the lock ineffective);
21	- a **once-per-process-per-conn guard** (``setup()`` is idempotent but re-locking + replaying
22	  the migration query on every call is wasteful), with an in-process lock making the
23	  check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads don't both run setup;
24	- **credential redaction**: a backend exception (psycopg/psycopg_pool) can embed the full
25	  ``user:password`` URI/DSN in its own message, so the conn string is scrubbed precisely and
26	  the exception text via URI-userinfo + DSN-password backstops; the redacted message is then
27	  raised *outside* the exception handler, so the credential-bearing original is retained as
28	  neither ``__cause__`` nor ``__context__`` (a logger walking ``__context__`` cannot
29	  re-expose it);
30	- a single shared **atexit** pool-cleanup handler (one per process, not one per call) so the
31	  pool's worker thread is joined before interpreter finalization.
32	
33	LangGraph/psycopg are optional (the ``persistence`` extra) and imported lazily; the
34	credential-redaction and connection-resolution logic is pure and unit-tested offline.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import atexit
40	import os
41	import re
42	import threading
43	from typing import Any, cast
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	from assay_engine._local import require_local_uri
47	
48	_DEFAULT_PG = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
49	
50	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes checkpoint
51	# schema migrations across concurrent initializers. The value is irrelevant beyond being a
52	# stable per-logical-schema constant; "ASSY" as an int.
53	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4153_5359
54	
55	# Upper bound (milliseconds) on how long the cross-process advisory lock may be waited for
56	# during schema bootstrap. A stuck/crashed peer that still holds a live PG session on the lock
57	# must NOT be able to hang bootstrap forever (#129); lock_timeout aborts the wait. 0 disables
58	# the bound (block indefinitely, the pre-#129 behavior).
59	_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS = max(0, int(os.environ.get("ASSAY_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS", "30000")))
60	
61	# Brief meta-lock guarding the process-local registries below (atexit registration, the open-pool
62	# list, the per-conn lock map, the pool cache). It is held ONLY for in-memory check-and-set — it
63	# is NEVER held across blocking cross-process I/O (pool.connection(), the advisory-lock wait, or
64	# setup() DDL). That blocking work is serialized per conn_str instead (#129), so a stuck advisory
65	# lock on one database cannot block initialization of an unrelated one.
66	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
67	
68	# Per-conn_str bootstrap locks: only same-conn_str initializers serialize; different databases
69	# bootstrap concurrently. Created lazily under _init_lock.
70	_CONN_INIT_LOCKS: dict[str, threading.Lock] = {}
71	
72	# Live pools cached by conn_str so the factory reuses one self-healing pool (and its worker
73	# thread) per database rather than opening a fresh pool on every call (#144).
74	_POOLS_BY_CONN: dict[str, Any] = {}
75	
76	# Pools opened by the factory, closed once at process exit via a single shared atexit handler
77	# (registering one handler per call would accumulate handlers across repeated factory calls).
78	_OPEN_POOLS: list[Any] = []
79	_atexit_registered = False
80	
81	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL) has already run in this
82	# process — bootstrap once per process per schema; the advisory lock still guards the
83	# first-time cross-process race.
84	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
85	
86	# Strips ``://user:password@`` (URI userinfo) from any URI-like substring. '/' is allowed
87	# inside the userinfo so a password containing '/' is still redacted; only '@' (the userinfo
88	# terminator) and whitespace bound the match.
89	_CREDS_RE = re.compile(r"://[^@\s]+@")
90	# Strips a libpq keyword/value DSN password token (``password=...`` / ``pgpassword=...``),
91	# handling quoted values that may contain spaces.
92	_DSN_PW_RE = re.compile(r"(?i)\b(password|pgpassword)\s*=\s*('[^']*'|\"[^\"]*\"|\S+)")
93	
94	
95	def _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str: str) -> str:
96	    """Return ``scheme://host[:port]/path`` with any ``user:password`` userinfo stripped.
97	
98	    Robust to malformed userinfo: a password containing ``/``/``#`` makes ``urlsplit``'s
99	    ``.port`` raise ``ValueError`` on access, so the whole parse (including attribute access)
100	    is guarded and falls back to the generic userinfo-strip regex / a constant placeholder.
101	    """
102	    try:
103	        u = urlsplit(conn_str)
104	        host = u.hostname or ""
105	        port = u.port
106	        scheme = u.scheme
107	        path = u.path
108	    except ValueError:
109	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str)
110	    if not host:
111	        return _CREDS_RE.sub("://", conn_str) if scheme else "<store>"
112	    if ":" in host:  # IPv6 literal — urlsplit drops the brackets; restore them
113	        host = f"[{host}]"
114	    netloc = f"{host}:{port}" if port else host
115	    return f"{scheme}://{netloc}{path}" if scheme else "<store>"
116	
117	
118	def redact_creds(text: str, conn_str: str = "") -> str:
119	    """Remove credentials a backend exception may embed in its message text.
120	
121	    Layers, in order of precision:
122	
123	    1. exact replacement of the known ``conn_str`` (handles any password characters, incl.
124	       ``/`` and spaces) — this is the precise layer and covers the real connection string;
125	    2. a generic URI-userinfo strip (``://user:pass@`` → ``://``) as a backstop for any
126	       *other* URI in the text;
127	    3. a libpq DSN ``password=``/``pgpassword=`` strip for the keyword/value form.
128	
129	    Layers 2–3 are best-effort for *secondary* endpoints (e.g. a replica DSN) the caller did
130	    not pass as ``conn_str``; the primary connection string is always scrubbed precisely.
131	    """
132	    if conn_str:
133	        text = text.replace(conn_str, _sanitize_conn_str(conn_str))
134	    text = _CREDS_RE.sub("://", text)
135	    return _DSN_PW_RE.sub(r"\1=***", text)
136	
137	
138	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
139	    """Resolve the Postgres connection string (``ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL`` env → loopback default).
140	
141	    The env path bypasses any external config validation, so loopback is enforced here at the
142	    point of use (ADR-0003 defense-in-depth): graph state must not leave the box.
143	    """
144	    url = os.environ.get("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL") or _DEFAULT_PG
145	    return require_local_uri(url, what="checkpointer connection")
146	
147	
148	def _safe_close(pool: Any) -> None:
149	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing ``close()`` does not abort
150	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (``close()`` calls a C ``finish()``
151	    that can raise)."""
152	    try:
153	        pool.close()
154	    except Exception:
155	        pass
156	
157	
158	def _close_all_pools() -> None:
159	    for p in _OPEN_POOLS:
160	        _safe_close(p)
161	
162	
163	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool: Any) -> None:
164	    global _atexit_registered
165	    with _init_lock:
166	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
167	        if not _atexit_registered:
168	            atexit.register(_close_all_pools)
169	            _atexit_registered = True
170	
171	
172	def _conn_init_lock(conn_str: str) -> threading.Lock:
173	    """Return the per-conn_str bootstrap lock, creating it under the brief meta-lock (#129)."""
174	    with _init_lock:
175	        lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS.get(conn_str)
176	        if lock is None:
177	            lock = _CONN_INIT_LOCKS[conn_str] = threading.Lock()
178	        return lock
179	
180	
181	def _cached_pool(conn_str: str) -> Any:
182	    with _init_lock:
183	        return _POOLS_BY_CONN.get(conn_str)
184	
185	
186	def _acquire_migration_lock(conn: Any) -> None:
187	    """Acquire the cross-process schema-migration advisory lock with a bounded wait (#129).
188	
189	    ``lock_timeout`` aborts the ``pg_advisory_lock`` wait if a peer holds it past the deadline,
190	    so a stuck/crashed initializer cannot hang bootstrap indefinitely; the resulting error
191	    propagates as a redacted ``RuntimeError`` like any other backend failure. set_config is used
192	    (not ``SET``) so the value can be safely parameterized.
193	    """
194	    if _MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS:
195	        conn.execute(
196	            "SELECT set_config('lock_timeout', %s, false)", (str(_MIGRATION_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS),)
197	        )
198	    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
199	
200	
201	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False) -> Any:
202	    """Return a configured LangGraph checkpointer.
203	
204	    ``use_memory=True`` returns an in-memory saver (tests / no-Postgres). Otherwise a
205	    Postgres-backed saver over a self-healing pool, with advisory-locked one-time schema
206	    setup. Raises ``RuntimeError`` (with credentials redacted) if the backend is unreachable
207	    or the extra is not installed.
208	    """
209	    try:
210	        if use_memory:
211	            from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
212	
213	            return MemorySaver()
214	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
215	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
216	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
217	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
218	        raise RuntimeError(
219	            "Postgres checkpointer requires the 'persistence' extra "
220	            "(langgraph-checkpoint-postgres, psycopg[binary,pool]) — not installed"
221	        ) from exc
222	
223	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
224	
225	    # Fast path: reuse a live, self-healing pool for this conn_str rather than opening a new pool
226	    # (and its background worker thread) on every call (#144). The liveness check on checkout
227	    # replaces dead connections, so a cached pool stays valid across transient outages.
228	    cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)
229	    if cached is not None:
230	        return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
231	
232	    # Serialize bootstrap PER conn_str — NOT process-globally (#129). Unrelated databases
233	    # initialize concurrently, and a stuck advisory lock on one conn_str can never block init of
234	    # another. The brief meta-lock (_init_lock) is only taken inside the helpers, never held
235	    # across the blocking advisory-lock wait / DDL below.
236	    with _conn_init_lock(conn_str):
237	        cached = _cached_pool(conn_str)  # another thread may have built it while we waited
238	        if cached is not None:
239	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, cached))
240	
241	        failure: str | None = None
242	        pool: Any = None
243	        try:
244	            pool = ConnectionPool(
245	                conn_str,
246	                min_size=1,
247	                max_size=8,
248	                kwargs={"autocommit": True, "prepare_threshold": 0, "row_factory": dict_row},
249	                check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,  # liveness check on checkout (self-healing)
250	                open=True,
251	            )
252	            _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
253	            # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. The advisory lock and the DDL
254	            # must run on the SAME connection (pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped); a pool-backed
255	            # setup() would lock one connection and DDL on another, leaving the lock ineffective.
256	            with pool.connection() as conn:
257	                _acquire_migration_lock(conn)  # bounded wait so a stuck peer can't hang us (#129)
258	                try:
259	                    # row_factory=dict_row makes this connection dict-rowed at runtime
260	                    # (what PostgresSaver requires); the cast asserts that runtime truth
261	                    # the type system can't infer from the pool kwargs.
262	                    PostgresSaver(cast(Any, conn)).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
263	                finally:
264	                    # Best-effort unlock; the lock auto-releases when the connection
265	                    # returns to the pool, and raising here would mask a setup() error.
266	                    try:
267	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
268	                    except Exception:
269	                        pass
270	            with _init_lock:
271	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
272	                _POOLS_BY_CONN[conn_str] = pool  # cache for reuse on later calls (#144)
273	            return PostgresSaver(cast(Any, pool))  # dict-rowed pool (row_factory=dict_row)
274	        except Exception as exc:
275	            # Build the redacted message here, but raise it OUTSIDE the handler (below) so the
276	            # original — credential-bearing — exception is retained as neither __cause__ NOR
277	            # __context__ (a logger walking __context__ would otherwise re-expose the password).
278	            failure = (
279	                f"Postgres checkpointer connection failed (is Postgres running on "
280	                f"{_sanitize_conn_str(conn_str)}?): {redact_creds(str(exc), conn_str)}. "
281	                "For tests without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
282	            )
283	            # The pool may have opened before bootstrap failed — close it and drop it from the
284	            # cleanup registry so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread (#105).
285	            if pool is not None:
286	                _safe_close(pool)
287	                with _init_lock:
288	                    if pool in _OPEN_POOLS:
289	                        _OPEN_POOLS.remove(pool)
290	        raise RuntimeError(failure)
291	
292	
293	def configured_checkpointer(use_memory: bool | None = None) -> Any:
294	    """Return the checkpointer the environment selects.
295	
296	    ``use_memory`` (or ``ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND=memory``) chooses the in-memory saver;
297	    otherwise Postgres.
298	    """
299	    if use_memory is None:
300	        use_memory = os.environ.get("ASSAY_CHECKPOINT_BACKEND", "postgres").lower() == "memory"
301	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=use_memory)
302

1	"""Canonical schema — the dataset-agnostic shape the baseline is built from.
2	
3	An adapter's job is to turn its raw source into a :class:`Corpus` of these types. The engine
4	builds the baseline from a ``Corpus`` and never sees the source's native format.
5	
6	Firewall A boundary (an adapter obligation, not a type-enforced guarantee):
7	    A ``Corpus`` carries the *data itself* — units of text/attributes and any structure that
8	    is intrinsic to the raw source. It must **not** carry an external authority's *judgments*
9	    about the data (asserted relationships, strengths, labels, a taxonomy). Those are claims;
10	    they travel through :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`, quarantined from baseline
11	    construction. Putting an external mapping into ``Corpus.relations`` (or external labels
12	    into ``attributes``/``metadata``) is a Firewall A violation.
13	
14	    Because the mapping/relation payloads are deliberately open (the engine is
15	    dataset-agnostic), this boundary is an **adapter contract obligation** the type system
16	    cannot enforce — not a structural guarantee. The structural guarantees of Firewall A are
17	    that the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` and that
18	    ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside adjudication mode (audit pass 1,
19	    issues #1, #3).
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import Any, Mapping
26	
27	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
31	class Unit:
32	    """One analyzable object in the corpus (a document, record, entity, item).
33	
34	    ``unit_id`` is stable and unique within the corpus. ``text`` is the natural-language
35	    content the NLP baseline operates on (may be empty for non-text units). ``attributes``
36	    holds structured fields intrinsic to the data — never an external authority's verdicts.
37	    """
38	
39	    unit_id: str
40	    text: str = ""
41	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
42	    source_ref: str | None = None
43	
44	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
45	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
46	
47	
48	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
49	class Relation:
50	    """A relationship that is *intrinsic to the raw data* (not an external judgment).
51	
52	    Example of legitimate use: a parent/child link the source file itself encodes. Example of
53	    illegitimate use: an external expert's asserted mapping between units — that is a claim
54	    (see :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`) and must be withheld from the baseline.
55	    """
56	
57	    source_id: str
58	    target_id: str
59	    kind: str
60	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
61	
62	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
63	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
64	
65	
66	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
67	class Corpus:
68	    """The full canonical dataset handed to the baseline builder."""
69	
70	    units: tuple[Unit, ...]
71	    relations: tuple[Relation, ...] = ()
72	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
73	
74	    def unit_ids(self) -> frozenset[str]:
75	        return frozenset(u.unit_id for u in self.units)
76	
77	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
78	        ids = [u.unit_id for u in self.units]
79	        if len(ids) != len(set(ids)):
80	            raise ValueError("Corpus contains duplicate unit_id values")
81	        # A closed canonical corpus must not contain relations to non-existent units
82	        # (audit pass 1, issue #3) — mirrors the duplicate-id and FeatureMatrix checks.
83	        known = set(ids)
84	        dangling = sorted(
85	            {e for r in self.relations for e in (r.source_id, r.target_id) if e not in known}
86	        )
87	        if dangling:
88	            raise ValueError(f"Corpus has relations referencing unknown unit_id(s): {dangling[:5]}")
89	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
90

1	"""Analysis-graph assembly + run/resume — the goal-agnostic orchestration wiring.
2	
3	The concrete nodes and topology of a study's analysis graph are the study's (adapter's)
4	responsibility — they encode dataset specifics and so must not live in the engine (ADR-0002).
5	The engine provides the reusable, hardened mechanics around them:
6	
7	- compile a LangGraph ``StateGraph`` with the durable checkpointer attached (so a run can
8	  interrupt at a gate and resume later);
9	- bound non-converging gate-rejection loops with a recursion limit, so a run that never
10	  converges fails loud (``GraphRecursionError``) instead of looping unbounded;
11	- stamp ``run_id`` as the checkpointer ``thread_id`` and onto traces (cross-store
12	  correlation), inside a trace context;
13	- the gate interrupt/resume protocol with its correlation guard (see
14	  :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode`).
15	
16	LangGraph is an optional extra (``orchestration``, ADR-0006), imported lazily.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	from typing import Any, Callable
22	
23	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import bootstrap_tracing, run_trace_context
24	
25	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds a gate-rejection→re-analysis loop so a
26	# non-converging run terminates loudly instead of hanging.
27	RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
28	
29	
30	def _require_langgraph() -> Any:
31	    try:
32	        import langgraph  # noqa: F401
33	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
34	        raise RuntimeError(
35	            "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
36	        ) from exc
37	    return langgraph
38	
39	
40	def compile_graph(
41	    state_schema: Any,
42	    *,
43	    build: Callable[[Any], None],
44	    checkpointer: Any | None = None,
45	    requires_checkpointer: bool = False,
46	) -> Any:
47	    """Compile a study's analysis graph.
48	
49	    ``build(builder)`` is a study-supplied callback that adds the study's nodes, edges, and
50	    entry point to the LangGraph ``StateGraph`` builder; the engine owns construction and
51	    attaching the durable ``checkpointer``. Returns the compiled graph.
52	
53	    Set ``requires_checkpointer=True`` for any graph containing gate nodes: ``interrupt`` is a
54	    silent no-op without a checkpointer, so a gate graph compiled without one would never park
55	    for the operator. The engine fails loud rather than ship a silently-broken governance
56	    graph (audit #G3). The engine cannot introspect the study-supplied ``build`` callback for
57	    gate nodes, so this is the caller's responsibility: **any study wiring**
58	    :func:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode.make_gate_node` **must pass
59	    ``requires_checkpointer=True``** (see GOVERNANCE.md "Gate interrupt/resume protocol").
60	
61	    Provenance note (#111): a gate node records its decision into LangGraph ``gate_decisions``
62	    state, which is **not** the hash-chained, tamper-evident trail. To get GOVERNANCE §3
63	    integrity on the durable path, pass ``make_gate_node`` a ``recorder`` that writes each
64	    decision into a :class:`~assay_engine.provenance.ProvenanceTrail`; without it, the graph
65	    path's gate decisions live only as graph state.
66	    """
67	    if requires_checkpointer and checkpointer is None:
68	        raise RuntimeError(
69	            "compile_graph(requires_checkpointer=True): a gate-bearing graph needs a durable "
70	            "checkpointer — interrupt() cannot park the run for the operator without one"
71	        )
72	    _require_langgraph()
73	    from langgraph.graph import StateGraph
74	
75	    builder = StateGraph(state_schema)
76	    build(builder)
77	    if checkpointer is not None:
78	        return builder.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
79	    return builder.compile()
80	
81	
82	def _thread_config(run_id: str, recursion_limit: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
83	    # Resume/run are keyed ONLY by thread_id — the checkpointer loads the latest (interrupted)
84	    # state itself. We deliberately do NOT thread a checkpoint_id: passing one is the
85	    # time-travel/replay API, and Command(resume=...) WITH a checkpoint_id replays from that
86	    # historical checkpoint instead of resuming the interrupt (version-dependent; LangGraph
87	    # issue #7361) — which would re-execute already-completed gates and break governance order.
88	    return {
89	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
90	        "recursion_limit": recursion_limit,
91	    }
92	
93	
94	def run(
95	    graph: Any,
96	    state: Any,
97	    *,
98	    run_id: str,
99	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
100	    trace: bool = True,
101	) -> Any:
102	    """Invoke a compiled graph for ``run_id`` (the checkpointer thread id), within a trace
103	    context, bounded by ``recursion_limit``. The run parks at the first gate ``interrupt``;
104	    resume it with :func:`resume`.
105	
106	    Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
107	    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
108	    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
109	    """
110	    if trace:
111	        bootstrap_tracing()
112	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
113	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
114	        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")
115	
116	
117	def resume(
118	    graph: Any,
119	    *,
120	    run_id: str,
121	    gate_id: str,
122	    decision: str,
123	    rationale: str,
124	    recursion_limit: int = RECURSION_LIMIT,
125	    trace: bool = True,
126	) -> Any:
127	    """Resume a run parked at a gate with the operator's decision.
128	
129	    ``gate_id`` is the gate the operator intended to decide (from the governance channel) and
130	    is REQUIRED — the gate node's correlation guard rejects it if it does not match the parked
131	    gate, so a stale/misrouted decision cannot resume the wrong gate. Must use the SAME
132	    checkpointer the run started with (``thread_id == run_id``); resume always continues the
133	    latest interrupted state for the thread (no checkpoint_id — see :func:`_thread_config`).
134	    """
135	    _require_langgraph()
136	    from langgraph.types import Command
137	
138	    if trace:
139	        bootstrap_tracing()
140	    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
141	    resume_value = {"gate_id": gate_id, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
142	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
143	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config, durability="sync")
144

1	"""Discovery runner — Firewall B (discover/confirm separation) enforced by construction.
2	
3	The mirror of :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.adjudication`. In discovery mode the data surfaces
4	candidate hypotheses; Firewall B (METHODOLOGY.md §2, ADR-0005) requires that the data used to
5	*discover* a hypothesis is not the data used to *confirm* it — otherwise discovering a pattern
6	and "testing" it on the same data proves nothing.
7	
8	The engine had the split primitive (:class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`) and a confirm step that
9	refuses discovery ids, but no composition — so the separation held only if a study wired it by
10	hand (same risk Firewall A had before ADR-0008). :func:`discover_and_confirm` makes it
11	structural: it hands the ``discover`` step a corpus containing *only* the discovery partition
12	and the ``confirm`` step a corpus containing *only* the held-out partition, so neither step can
13	accidentally use the other's data.
14	
15	Honest scope (lesson from the adjudication review, ADR-0008): the engine partitions the
16	**structural** data — units, and relations whose *both* endpoints are in the partition. It
17	does NOT carry the corpus's free-form ``metadata`` into a subset (an arbitrary mapping cannot
18	be partitioned safely — a per-unit index in metadata would otherwise leak the other partition
19	wholesale), and it cannot police free-text inside ``Unit``/``Relation.attributes`` that an
20	adapter might use to reference units outside the partition. So: the structural separation is
21	enforced; keeping per-unit data out of metadata/attribute *payloads* is the adapter's
22	obligation (the Firewall-B analogue of the corpus-content caveat in ADR-0005). And, as with
23	adjudication, this is a *signature-level* guarantee — it prevents accidental double-dipping,
24	not a step that willfully reflects into the caller's frame (only process isolation could).
25	"""
26	
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import datetime as _dt
30	from typing import Callable, Iterable, Protocol
31	
32	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
33	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
34	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
35	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
36	    TimestampAuthority,
37	    require_preregistered,
38	)
39	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
40	
41	
42	class HeldOutConfirmer(Protocol):
43	    """Study-supplied: confirm one locked, data-surfaced hypothesis on the held-out corpus."""
44	
45	    def __call__(self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, held_out: Corpus) -> Verdict: ...
46	
47	
48	def subset_corpus(corpus: Corpus, ids: Iterable[str]) -> Corpus:
49	    """Return the sub-corpus of ``corpus`` restricted to ``ids``.
50	
51	    Units not in ``ids`` are dropped; a relation is kept only if *both* endpoints are in
52	    ``ids`` (a relation to a unit outside the partition would leak that unit's existence/links
53	    into the partition).
54	
55	    Corpus-level ``metadata`` is deliberately **dropped**, not carried through: it is an
56	    arbitrary mapping the engine cannot partition safely. A per-unit index living in metadata
57	    (e.g. ``{"by_unit": {unit_id: ...}}``) would otherwise hand the discovery step the held-out
58	    partition wholesale — silently defeating Firewall B. Adapters that need partition-scoped
59	    side data must put it on the ``Unit``/``Relation`` records (which *are* partitioned), not in
60	    corpus metadata; free-text inside ``attributes`` that references foreign units remains the
61	    adapter's obligation (ADR-0005 / ADR-0008 honest-scope caveat).
62	    """
63	    keep = frozenset(ids)
64	    units = tuple(u for u in corpus.units if u.unit_id in keep)
65	    relations = tuple(r for r in corpus.relations if r.source_id in keep and r.target_id in keep)
66	    return Corpus(units=units, relations=relations)
67	
68	
69	def discover_and_confirm(
70	    corpus: Corpus,
71	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
72	    *,
73	    discover: Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]],
74	    confirm: HeldOutConfirmer,
75	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
76	) -> list[Verdict]:
77	    """Run discovery then confirmation with the data separation enforced by construction.
78	
79	    1. ``discover`` is handed a corpus containing only the discovery partition and returns
80	       candidate hypotheses. They must be ``DISCOVERY``-origin and genuinely **pre-registered**
81	       before confirmation — ``require_preregistered`` checks (via ``authority``) that the proof
82	       binds each hypothesis's content and that its attested lock time precedes confirmation.
83	       Discovering and confirming on the same data, or confirming a hypothesis whose content was
84	       changed after locking, is the double-dipping Firewall B exists to prevent.
85	    2. ``confirm`` is handed a corpus containing only the held-out partition and tests each
86	       hypothesis there; the verdict must report the hypothesis it answers.
87	
88	    ``authority`` is the pre-registration timestamp authority (e.g.
89	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.LocalHmacAuthority`, or an RFC-3161
90	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default. A study's ``discover`` step locks each
91	    discovered hypothesis (e.g. via :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.
92	    lock_hypothesis`) before returning it.
93	
94	    Returns the verdicts. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl. ``PreRegistrationError``) on an
95	    empty discovery or held-out partition (split ids that select no corpus units — a vacuous
96	    test), a non-``DISCOVERY`` hypothesis, a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered
97	    before confirmation, or a verdict↔hypothesis misattribution.
98	    """
99	    discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, split.discovery_ids)
100	    if not discovery_corpus.units:
101	        # the discovery partition selects no actual corpus units — a hypothesis "discovered"
102	        # from zero data is not data-surfaced at all; it would confirm cleanly on the held-out
103	        # set and silently launder a hand-authored claim through Firewall B. Fail loud.
104	        raise FirewallViolation(
105	            "discovery partition selects no corpus units — nothing to discover from "
106	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
107	        )
108	    hypotheses = list(discover(discovery_corpus))  # sees only the discovery partition
109	
110	    held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, split.confirm_ids)  # disjoint from discovery (split invariant)
111	    if not held_out.units:
112	        # the confirmation partition selects no actual corpus units — confirming on an empty
113	        # held-out set is a vacuous test (the split ids don't match the corpus). Fail loud.
114	        raise FirewallViolation(
115	            "confirmation partition selects no corpus units — nothing to confirm on "
116	            "(do split ids match the corpus unit ids?)"
117	        )
118	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
119	    for hypothesis in hypotheses:
120	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
121	            raise FirewallViolation(
122	                f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is {hypothesis.origin.value!r}; the "
123	                "discovery runner expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
124	            )
125	        # Pre-registration enforced by the runner: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content
126	        # (no post-lock content swap) and its attested lock time must precede this confirmation.
127	        require_preregistered(
128	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
129	        )
130	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, held_out)
131	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
132	            raise FirewallViolation(
133	                f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for hypothesis "
134	                f"{hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
135	            )
136	        verdicts.append(verdict)
137	    return verdicts
138

1	"""Typed, falsifiable hypotheses (METHODOLOGY.md §3).
2	
3	Every hypothesis names what is claimed, the test, the data it runs on, and a pre-specified
4	decision rule. Two origins:
5	
6	- ``DISCOVERY`` — surfaced by the data, then made specific, locked, and timestamped before
7	  confirmation (Firewall B).
8	- ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` — derived by the adapter from an external expert claim, tested against
9	  the blind baseline (Firewall A).
10	
11	Two kinds, which select the confirmation mechanism:
12	
13	- ``UNIT_LEVEL`` — confirmed on a held-out split.
14	- ``WHOLE_CORPUS`` — no held-out object possible; confirmed against null/permutation
15	  distributions and stability across resamples.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
21	from enum import Enum
22	from typing import Any, Literal, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
25	
26	# The tail a claim predicts the statistic lies in, relative to the null.
27	Direction = Literal["greater", "less"]
28	
29	
30	class HypothesisKind(Enum):
31	    UNIT_LEVEL = "unit_level"
32	    WHOLE_CORPUS = "whole_corpus"
33	
34	
35	class HypothesisOrigin(Enum):
36	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
37	    EXTERNAL_CLAIM = "external_claim"
38	
39	
40	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
41	class Hypothesis:
42	    """A locked, falsifiable assertion ready for confirmatory testing.
43	
44	    ``locked_at`` and ``timestamp_proof`` are populated when the hypothesis is sealed for
45	    pre-registration. An unlocked hypothesis (either field ``None``) must not be passed to a
46	    confirmatory test.
47	
48	    Scope of ``locked`` (audit pass 1, issue #6): ``locked`` is only a cheap **presence**
49	    predicate — both fields populated. It is NOT the methodology-grade check and carries no
50	    cryptographic meaning on its own. The real pre-registration verification —
51	    content-binding (the proof covers this hypothesis's content), timestamp attestation, and
52	    lock-before-confirm ordering — lives in
53	    :mod:`assay_engine.methodology.preregistration` (``verify_preregistration`` /
54	    ``require_preregistered``), and the confirmatory runners (:mod:`adjudication`,
55	    :mod:`discovery`) enforce it via a supplied ``TimestampAuthority``. Use
56	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.lock_hypothesis` to produce a hypothesis
57	    whose lock actually verifies; a hand-set ``timestamp_proof`` string satisfies ``locked``
58	    but will fail ``verify_preregistration``.
59	    """
60	
61	    hypothesis_id: str
62	    statement: str
63	    kind: HypothesisKind
64	    origin: HypothesisOrigin
65	    test_name: str
66	    decision_rule: str
67	    source_claim_id: str | None = None
68	    locked_at: str | None = None
69	    timestamp_proof: str | None = None
70	    # The predicted direction, fixed at pre-registration so it cannot be chosen post-hoc
71	    # after seeing the baseline (audit pass 2, issue #24). For an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis
72	    # the adapter derives this from the claim's assertion, not from the baseline.
73	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None
74	    params: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
75	
76	    @property
77	    def locked(self) -> bool:
78	        return self.locked_at is not None and self.timestamp_proof is not None
79	
80	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
81	        if self.origin is HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM and not self.source_claim_id:
82	            raise ValueError("EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypotheses must carry a source_claim_id")
83	        object.__setattr__(self, "params", freeze_mapping(self.params))
84

1	"""Confirmatory-test machinery (METHODOLOGY.md §2, §4).
2	
3	A confirmatory test takes a *locked* hypothesis and returns a :class:`Verdict`. Two
4	mechanisms, selected by hypothesis kind:
5	
6	- unit-level → evaluate on a held-out confirmation partition (Firewall B via
7	  :class:`DiscoverConfirmSplit`);
8	- whole-corpus → compare the observed statistic against a null/permutation distribution and
9	  require stability across resamples.
10	
11	This module defines the *contract and guards* around confirmation. The concrete statistics
12	are supplied by callers via the baseline toolkit; the engine's responsibility here is to
13	refuse to confirm an unlocked hypothesis or on discovery data, validate the supplied
14	statistics, and map an outcome onto one of the three verdicts honestly (including
15	``indeterminate``).
16	
17	Direction handling (audit pass 1, issue #2): a claim predicts the statistic lies in a
18	particular tail of the null. The empirical p-value is computed against *that* tail, so a
19	claim predicting a low value is confirmable, and a genuine contradiction (significant in the
20	opposite tail) is detectable — both were impossible under the previous direction-blind
21	upper-tail-only implementation.
22	"""
23	
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import datetime as _dt
27	import math
28	from typing import Iterable, Sequence
29	
30	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit, FirewallViolation
31	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Direction, Hypothesis, HypothesisKind
32	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
33	    TimestampAuthority,
34	    require_preregistered,
35	)
36	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
37	
38	
39	def _gate_preregistration(hypothesis: Hypothesis, authority: TimestampAuthority | None) -> None:
40	    """Pre-registration gate for the confirm primitives.
41	
42	    If ``authority`` is supplied, run the full :func:`require_preregistered` check (content
43	    binding + verifiable timestamp + lock-before-this-moment) — so a study calling a confirm
44	    primitive *directly* (outside a runner) gets the same guarantee the runners enforce. If it
45	    is ``None``, fall back to the cheap presence gate (:func:`require_locked`); the caller has
46	    then opted out of real verification and owns that choice.
47	    """
48	    if authority is not None:
49	        require_preregistered(
50	            hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=_dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
51	        )
52	    else:
53	        require_locked(hypothesis)
54	
55	
56	def require_locked(hypothesis: Hypothesis) -> None:
57	    """Cheap **presence** gate: refuse a hypothesis that carries no lock fields at all.
58	
59	    This is defense-in-depth, not the methodology-grade check. It does not verify the proof
60	    binds the content or that the lock precedes confirmation — that is
61	    :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.require_preregistered`, which the
62	    confirmatory *runners* (:mod:`adjudication`, :mod:`discovery`) call before they confirm.
63	    A study driving :func:`confirm_whole_corpus`/:func:`confirm_unit_level` directly (outside a
64	    runner) should call ``require_preregistered`` itself to get the real guarantee.
65	    """
66	    if not hypothesis.locked:
67	        raise FirewallViolation(
68	            f"hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} is not locked; confirm is forbidden "
69	            "before pre-registration (Firewall B / pre-registration)"
70	        )
71	
72	
73	def _validate_alpha(alpha: float) -> None:
74	    # A significance threshold of 0.5 or more is not a meaningful one-sided level, and
75	    # admitting it lets both tails register "significant" at once (alpha < 0.5 guarantees
76	    # p_support + p_contra >= 1 cannot both fall below alpha) — fix-review nit on issue #2.
77	    if not (0.0 < alpha < 0.5):
78	        raise ValueError(f"alpha must be in (0, 0.5); got {alpha}")
79	
80	
81	def _validate_pvalue(p_value: float) -> None:
82	    if not (0.0 <= p_value <= 1.0):
83	        raise ValueError(f"p_value must be in [0, 1]; got {p_value}")
84	
85	
86	def _validate_finite(name: str, value: float) -> None:
87	    if not math.isfinite(value):
88	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must be a finite number; got {value}")
89	
90	
91	def verdict_from_pvalue(
92	    hypothesis_id: str,
93	    *,
94	    statistic: float,
95	    p_value: float,
96	    alpha: float,
97	    powered: bool,
98	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
99	    test_description: str = "confirmatory test",
100	) -> Verdict:
101	    """Map a confirmatory statistic onto the three verdicts.
102	
103	    - not ``powered`` → ``indeterminate`` (the method cannot decide).
104	    - significant and direction agrees with the claim → ``supported``.
105	    - significant and direction disagrees → ``contradicted``.
106	    - not significant → ``indeterminate`` (absence of evidence is not contradiction here).
107	
108	    ``p_value`` and ``direction_supports_claim`` must be derived by the caller from a test
109	    appropriate to the claim's predicted direction. For a unit-level held-out test the engine
110	    is handed only an opaque ``statistic`` + ``p_value`` and cannot itself infer whether the
111	    statistic supports or contradicts the claim — so ``direction_supports_claim`` is a trusted
112	    caller obligation, ``statistic`` is recorded for provenance only (it does NOT gate the
113	    verdict), and the deciding flag is recorded in ``evidence`` so the verdict is re-derivable
114	    (audit pass 2, issue #20). Inputs are validated (issue #4) and the recorded decision rule
115	    reflects the caller's ``test_description``, not a hard-coded sidedness label (issue #2).
116	    """
117	    _validate_pvalue(p_value)
118	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
119	    _validate_finite("statistic", statistic)
120	
121	    rule = f"{test_description} at alpha={alpha}; powered={powered}"
122	    common = dict(
123	        statistic=statistic,
124	        threshold=alpha,
125	        evidence={"p_value": p_value, "direction_supports_claim": direction_supports_claim},
126	    )
127	    if not powered:
128	        return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="underpowered", **common)
129	    if p_value <= alpha:
130	        if direction_supports_claim:
131	            return Verdict.supported(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
132	        return Verdict.contradicted(hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
133	    return Verdict.indeterminate(hypothesis_id, rule, notes="not significant at alpha", **common)
134	
135	
136	def confirm_unit_level(
137	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
138	    *,
139	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit,
140	    evaluated_ids: Iterable[str],
141	    statistic: float,
142	    p_value: float,
143	    alpha: float,
144	    powered: bool,
145	    direction_supports_claim: bool,
146	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
147	) -> Verdict:
148	    """Confirm a unit-level hypothesis on the held-out partition only.
149	
150	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
151	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
152	    """
153	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.UNIT_LEVEL:
154	        raise ValueError("confirm_unit_level requires a UNIT_LEVEL hypothesis")
155	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
156	    split.assert_confirm_only(evaluated_ids)  # Firewall B (rejects empty / discovery ids)
157	    return verdict_from_pvalue(
158	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
159	        statistic=statistic,
160	        p_value=p_value,
161	        alpha=alpha,
162	        powered=powered,
163	        direction_supports_claim=direction_supports_claim,
164	        test_description="held-out unit-level test",
165	    )
166	
167	
168	def _empirical_p(null: Sequence[float], observed: float, tail: Direction) -> float:
169	    """One-sided empirical p-value with +1 smoothing for the given tail."""
170	    n = len(null)
171	    if tail == "greater":
172	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v >= observed)
173	    else:
174	        extreme = sum(1 for v in null if v <= observed)
175	    return (extreme + 1) / (n + 1)
176	
177	
178	def _resample_stability(
179	    resamples: Sequence[float], null: Sequence[float], tail: Direction, alpha: float
180	) -> float:
181	    """Fraction of resamples that **reproduce the observed effect**.
182	
183	    Each resample is scored as its own observed statistic against the null and must itself be
184	    significant in the predicted tail (empirical p ≤ ``alpha``). This measures reproduction of
185	    the observed *effect* — not merely landing on the predicted side of the null center, which a
186	    resample distribution clearing the null median by an epsilon would satisfy while reproducing
187	    nothing of the observed magnitude (audit pass 2, #139). The closer to 1.0, the more
188	    consistently the effect's significance reproduces across resamples.
189	    """
190	    significant = sum(1 for r in resamples if _empirical_p(null, r, tail) <= alpha)
191	    return significant / len(resamples)
192	
193	
194	_VALID_DIRECTIONS = ("greater", "less")
195	
196	
197	def _resolve_direction(hypothesis: Hypothesis, predicted_direction: Direction | None) -> Direction:
198	    """Direction is fixed at lock time on the hypothesis (issue #24); the confirm-time
199	    argument is optional and, if given, must match the pre-registered one.
200	
201	    The resolved direction is validated against ``{"greater", "less"}`` and any other value is
202	    rejected: an unrecognized direction would otherwise fall through to the 'less' tail in
203	    :func:`_empirical_p`, silently flipping supported↔contradicted (audit pass 2, #128).
204	    """
205	    locked_dir = hypothesis.predicted_direction
206	    if locked_dir is not None:
207	        if predicted_direction is not None and predicted_direction != locked_dir:
208	            raise ValueError(
209	                "predicted_direction argument contradicts the hypothesis's pre-registered "
210	                f"direction ({predicted_direction!r} != {locked_dir!r})"
211	            )
212	        resolved = locked_dir
213	    elif predicted_direction is None:
214	        raise ValueError(
215	            "no predicted_direction: set it on the hypothesis at pre-registration "
216	            "(preferred) or pass it explicitly"
217	        )
218	    else:
219	        resolved = predicted_direction
220	    if resolved not in _VALID_DIRECTIONS:
221	        raise ValueError(
222	            f"predicted_direction must be one of {_VALID_DIRECTIONS}; got {resolved!r} — an "
223	            "unrecognized tail would silently flip the supported/contradicted verdict"
224	        )
225	    return resolved
226	
227	
228	def confirm_whole_corpus(
229	    hypothesis: Hypothesis,
230	    *,
231	    observed: float,
232	    null_distribution: Sequence[float],
233	    alpha: float,
234	    resample_statistics: Sequence[float] | None = None,
235	    stability_threshold: float = 0.9,
236	    predicted_direction: Direction | None = None,
237	    authority: TimestampAuthority | None = None,
238	) -> Verdict:
239	    """Confirm a whole-corpus hypothesis against a null distribution + measured stability.
240	
241	    Pass ``authority`` to verify pre-registration in full (content binding + timestamp +
242	    lock-before-now); omit it to fall back to the presence gate (see :func:`require_locked`).
243	
244	    The predicted tail is taken from the hypothesis's pre-registered ``predicted_direction``
245	    (issue #24). The pattern must (a) beat the null at ``alpha`` in the predicted tail and
246	    (b) be **stable across resamples** — measured by the engine from ``resample_statistics``,
247	    not asserted by the caller (audit pass 2, issue #21). Outcomes:
248	
249	    - significant in the predicted tail AND stable → ``supported``;
250	    - significant in the *opposite* tail → ``contradicted``;
251	    - unstable, not significant, or stability not assessed → ``indeterminate``.
252	
253	    If ``resample_statistics`` is omitted, stability cannot be measured, so ``supported`` is
254	    unavailable and the verdict is ``indeterminate`` ("stability not assessed").
255	    """
256	    if hypothesis.kind is not HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS:
257	        raise ValueError("confirm_whole_corpus requires a WHOLE_CORPUS hypothesis")
258	    _gate_preregistration(hypothesis, authority)
259	    _validate_alpha(alpha)
260	    _validate_finite("observed", observed)
261	    if not null_distribution:
262	        raise ValueError(
263	            "a null/permutation distribution is required for whole-corpus confirmation"
264	        )
265	    for v in null_distribution:
266	        _validate_finite("null_distribution value", v)
267	    if not (0.0 < stability_threshold <= 1.0):
268	        raise ValueError(f"stability_threshold must be in (0, 1]; got {stability_threshold}")
269	
270	    direction = _resolve_direction(hypothesis, predicted_direction)
271	    opposite: Direction = "less" if direction == "greater" else "greater"
272	    p_support = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, direction)
273	    p_contra = _empirical_p(null_distribution, observed, opposite)
274	    n = len(null_distribution)
275	
276	    stability: float | None = None
277	    if resample_statistics is not None:
278	        if not resample_statistics:
279	            raise ValueError("resample_statistics, if given, must be non-empty")
280	        for r in resample_statistics:
281	            _validate_finite("resample statistic", r)
282	        stability = _resample_stability(resample_statistics, null_distribution, direction, alpha)
283	    stable = stability is not None and stability >= stability_threshold
284	
285	    rule = (
286	        f"empirical p from {n}-sample null at alpha={alpha}, predicted tail '{direction}'; "
287	        f"stability across resamples >= {stability_threshold} (engine-measured)"
288	    )
289	    evidence = {
290	        "p_support": p_support,
291	        "p_contradict": p_contra,
292	        "null_n": n,
293	        "predicted_direction": direction,
294	        "stability": stability,
295	        "stability_threshold": stability_threshold,
296	        "n_resamples": len(resample_statistics) if resample_statistics is not None else 0,
297	    }
298	    common = dict(statistic=observed, threshold=alpha, evidence=evidence)
299	
300	    # A contradiction (significant in the opposite tail) is reportable without stability.
301	    if p_contra <= alpha:
302	        return Verdict.contradicted(
303	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="significant in the opposite tail", **common
304	        )
305	    if stability is None:
306	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
307	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id,
308	            rule,
309	            notes="stability not assessed (no resamples supplied)",
310	            **common,
311	        )
312	    if not stable:
313	        return Verdict.indeterminate(
314	            hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="unstable across resamples", **common
315	        )
316	    if p_support <= alpha:
317	        return Verdict.supported(hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, **common)
318	    return Verdict.indeterminate(
319	        hypothesis.hypothesis_id, rule, notes="does not beat null at alpha", **common
320	    )
321

1	"""Adjudication runner — the engine owns the firewall guarantee, not each study (ADR-0008).
2	
3	METHODOLOGY.md describes the adjudication pipeline: build a baseline *blind* to the external
4	claims (Firewall A), convert each claim into a typed, pre-stated hypothesis, test each against
5	the blind baseline, and *score the external source* against the validated baseline (§5). Until
6	now the engine shipped the parts (a custodial :class:`ClaimBlindGuard`, the confirmatory tests,
7	the verdicts) but no composition — so Firewall A held only if a study wired it correctly by
8	hand. For a blueprint inherited by many studies, that pushes the single most important
9	methodological guarantee onto every cloner, where it is easy to get silently wrong (build the
10	baseline with the claims in scope and nothing stops you).
11	
12	:func:`adjudicate` makes the guarantee structural: it builds the baseline *inside* a sealed
13	claim-guard that holds the claims source, so the baseline builder cannot reach the claims
14	during construction (any attempt raises ``FirewallViolation``); only after the baseline is
15	built does the runner release the claims and adjudicate. It then aggregates the verdicts into a
16	:class:`SourceScorecard` — the §5 "score the source" step, expressed as alignment *frequency*
17	on decisive claims, explicitly NOT a normative judgement of the source's quality (richer
18	dimensions — where it aligns, diverges, leaves gaps — are data-surfaced downstream, per §5).
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import datetime as _dt
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Callable, Iterable, Protocol
26	
27	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, ExternalClaimsSource
28	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
29	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard, FirewallViolation
30	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
31	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import (
32	    TimestampAuthority,
33	    require_preregistered,
34	)
35	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict, VerdictLabel
36	
37	if TYPE_CHECKING:
38	    # Annotations only — importing baseline at runtime would invert the layering
39	    # (baseline.toolkit depends on methodology.firewalls) and create a cycle.
40	    from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
41	
42	
43	class ClaimConfirmer(Protocol):
44	    """Study-supplied: confirm one claim-derived hypothesis against the (blind) baseline.
45	
46	    The study owns this because only it knows the baseline's structure and how a given claim
47	    maps onto a measurement of it; the engine supplies the confirmatory primitives
48	    (``confirm_whole_corpus`` / ``confirm_unit_level``) the study uses inside it.
49	    """
50	
51	    def __call__(
52	        self, hypothesis: Hypothesis, baseline: BaselineArtifact, claim: ClaimRecord
53	    ) -> Verdict: ...
54	
55	
56	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
57	class SourceScorecard:
58	    """How an external source aligns with the independent baseline (METHODOLOGY.md §5).
59	
60	    ``alignment_rate`` is supported / (supported + contradicted) — the fraction of *decisive*
61	    claims where the blind baseline agrees with the source's assertion. ``indeterminate`` is
62	    excluded from the denominator (the method could not decide; counting it would understate
63	    or overstate alignment). This is a frequency, not a verdict on whether the source is
64	    "good": it says how often an independent reading of the data corroborates the source.
65	    """
66	
67	    source: str
68	    n_supported: int
69	    n_contradicted: int
70	    n_indeterminate: int
71	    alignment_rate: float | None
72	    verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = field(default_factory=tuple)
73	
74	    @property
75	    def total(self) -> int:
76	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted + self.n_indeterminate
77	
78	    @property
79	    def decisive(self) -> int:
80	        return self.n_supported + self.n_contradicted
81	
82	
83	def _score(source: str, verdicts: list[Verdict]) -> SourceScorecard:
84	    counts = {
85	        VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED: 0,
86	        VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED: 0,
87	        VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE: 0,
88	    }
89	    for v in verdicts:
90	        counts[v.label] += 1
91	    decisive = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] + counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED]
92	    rate = counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED] / decisive if decisive else None
93	    return SourceScorecard(
94	        source=source,
95	        n_supported=counts[VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED],
96	        n_contradicted=counts[VerdictLabel.CONTRADICTED],
97	        n_indeterminate=counts[VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE],
98	        alignment_rate=rate,
99	        verdicts=tuple(verdicts),
100	    )
101	
102	
103	def adjudicate(
104	    corpus: Corpus,
105	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource,
106	    *,
107	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder,
108	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
109	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
110	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
111	    source_name: str = "external",
112	) -> tuple[BaselineArtifact, SourceScorecard]:
113	    """Run the full adjudication, enforcing Firewall A by construction.
114	
115	    1. The claims source is kept in this function's local scope and is NOT handed to the
116	       builder; the baseline is built inside a sealed :class:`ClaimBlindGuard` that holds
117	       nothing. This is a **signature-level** guarantee: the builder is never *handed* a
118	       claims source by the engine, so it cannot accidentally consult the claims. It is NOT
119	       frame isolation — a builder that deliberately reflects into this runner's call stack
120	       (``sys._getframe``) could reach ``claims_source``; preventing that would require
121	       running the builder in a separate process, which is out of scope. The guarantee is
122	       against *accidental* circularity, not against a builder that willfully defeats the
123	       firewall.
124	    2. After the baseline exists, each claim is mapped (by ``hypothesis_for``) to its typed,
125	       pre-stated ``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` hypothesis whose ``source_claim_id`` must match the claim
126	       and which must be genuinely **pre-registered** — :func:`~assay_engine.methodology.
127	       preregistration.require_preregistered` checks (via the supplied ``authority``) that the
128	       proof binds this hypothesis's content and that its attested lock time precedes the
129	       **baseline build** (the ordering instant is captured before the build). A claim-derived
130	       hypothesis must therefore be locked *before* ``adjudicate`` is called — ``hypothesis_for``
131	       looks up an already-locked hypothesis; locking on demand inside it (after the baseline
132	       exists) is rejected, so the hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline.
133	    3. The verdict's ``hypothesis_id`` must match the hypothesis it answers (no misattribution),
134	       and the verdicts are aggregated into a :class:`SourceScorecard`.
135	
136	    ``authority`` is the pre-registration timestamp authority (e.g.
137	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.LocalHmacAuthority`, or an RFC-3161
138	    adapter); the engine ships no silent-accept default, so a study must supply a real one.
139	
140	    Returns ``(baseline, scorecard)``. Raises ``FirewallViolation`` (incl.
141	    :class:`~assay_engine.methodology.preregistration.PreRegistrationError`) on any
142	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity mismatch, a non-``EXTERNAL_CLAIM`` (data-surfaced)
143	    hypothesis, or a hypothesis that is not verifiably pre-registered before confirmation.
144	    """
145	    # The claims source stays in THIS function's local scope and is never given to the builder.
146	    # The builder receives a guard holding nothing (a blind-mode signal only), so there is no
147	    # object reachable from the builder that contains the claims — not even via the guard's
148	    # internals. This is stronger than a custodial guard, whose private `_claims_source` a
149	    # determined builder could read past the sealed `release()` check (adversarial review).
150	    # Captured BEFORE the baseline is built: an EXTERNAL_CLAIM hypothesis derives from the
151	    # external claim, not from the baseline, so it must be pre-registered before the baseline (the
152	    # answer) exists. Using this as `not_after` makes "lock before the result is in hand" real —
153	    # a hypothesis locked on demand inside `hypothesis_for` (i.e. after the build) is rejected,
154	    # forcing studies to pre-lock their claim-derived hypotheses (#83).
155	    not_after = _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc)
156	
157	    guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource] = ClaimBlindGuard()
158	    with guard.sealed():
159	        baseline = baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
160	
161	    scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
162	        baseline,
163	        claims_source.claims(),
164	        hypothesis_for=hypothesis_for,
165	        confirm=confirm,
166	        authority=authority,
167	        not_after=not_after,
168	        source_name=source_name,
169	    )
170	    return baseline, scorecard
171	
172	
173	def adjudicate_with_baseline(
174	    baseline: BaselineArtifact,
175	    claims: "Iterable[ClaimRecord]",
176	    *,
177	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis],
178	    confirm: ClaimConfirmer,
179	    authority: TimestampAuthority,
180	    not_after: _dt.datetime,
181	    source_name: str = "external",
182	    on_step: "Callable[[Hypothesis, Verdict], None] | None" = None,
183	) -> SourceScorecard:
184	    """Adjudicate a **materialized claim set** against an already-built blind baseline (core).
185	
186	    Factored out of :func:`adjudicate` so a composed pipeline that builds the baseline once
187	    (and shares it with discovery) can reuse the identical, hardened Firewall-A loop instead of
188	    rebuilding the baseline. The caller is responsible for having built ``baseline`` blind and
189	    for passing the ``not_after`` ordering instant captured *before* that build — so the
190	    lock-before-baseline guarantee is preserved across the shared-baseline composition.
191	
192	    ``claims`` is **materialized once** here (not re-pulled from a source): a caller that gated
193	    or recorded a claim snapshot must adjudicate *that* snapshot, not a set a re-iterable source
194	    could change between the gate and the scoring (adversarial review #95). Enforces the same
195	    claim↔hypothesis↔verdict identity + pre-registration checks as :func:`adjudicate`.
196	    """
197	    verdicts: list[Verdict] = []
198	    materialized = list(claims)  # claims used only after the blind baseline is built
199	    # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would be counted once per occurrence in
200	    # _score, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing alignment_rate (#138).
201	    seen_claim_ids: set[str] = set()
202	    for c in materialized:
203	        if c.claim_id in seen_claim_ids:
204	            raise FirewallViolation(
205	                f"duplicate claim_id {c.claim_id!r} within one adjudication run — claim identity "
206	                "must be unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator)"
207	            )
208	        seen_claim_ids.add(c.claim_id)
209	    for claim in materialized:
210	        hypothesis = hypothesis_for(claim)
211	        if hypothesis.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM:
212	            raise FirewallViolation(
213	                f"claim {claim.claim_id!r} produced a {hypothesis.origin.value!r} hypothesis; "
214	                "adjudicated claims must be EXTERNAL_CLAIM (pre-stated, not data-surfaced)"
215	            )
216	        if hypothesis.source_claim_id != claim.claim_id:
217	            raise FirewallViolation(
218	                f"hypothesis for claim {claim.claim_id!r} carries source_claim_id "
219	                f"{hypothesis.source_claim_id!r} — claim↔hypothesis misattribution"
220	            )
221	        # Pre-registration: the proof must bind THIS hypothesis's content (no post-lock content
222	        # swap) and its attested lock time must precede the baseline build (so the claim-derived
223	        # hypothesis cannot have been tuned to the baseline).
224	        require_preregistered(hypothesis, authority=authority, not_after=not_after)
225	        verdict = confirm(hypothesis, baseline, claim)
226	        if verdict.hypothesis_id != hypothesis.hypothesis_id:
227	            raise FirewallViolation(
228	                f"verdict for hypothesis {hypothesis.hypothesis_id!r} reports hypothesis_id "
229	                f"{verdict.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
230	            )
231	        verdicts.append(verdict)
232	        if on_step is not None:
233	            # per-claim hook (in order) so a caller can record the pre-registered hypothesis AND
234	            # its verdict to provenance as each happens, not in a post-hoc batch (#93).
235	            on_step(hypothesis, verdict)
236	
237	    return _score(source_name, verdicts)
238

1	"""Feature-builder interface — optional dataset-specific features.
2	
3	The baseline toolkit provides generic builders (embeddings, similarity, etc.). When a
4	dataset has features only meaningful in its own domain, an adapter supplies them through a
5	:class:`FeatureBuilder`. Output is a plain numeric :class:`FeatureMatrix` keyed by unit id,
6	so the engine consumes it without knowing what the features mean.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	from typing import Protocol, Sequence
13	
14	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
15	
16	
17	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
18	class FeatureMatrix:
19	    """Numeric features for a set of units. ``rows[i]`` corresponds to ``unit_ids[i]``."""
20	
21	    unit_ids: tuple[str, ...]
22	    feature_names: tuple[str, ...]
23	    rows: tuple[tuple[float, ...], ...]
24	
25	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
26	        if len(self.rows) != len(self.unit_ids):
27	            raise ValueError("FeatureMatrix row count must match unit_ids length")
28	        width = len(self.feature_names)
29	        if any(len(r) != width for r in self.rows):
30	            raise ValueError("FeatureMatrix rows must match feature_names width")
31	        # Enforce the numeric type contract the docstring promises: a non-numeric cell (a str,
32	        # None, NaN) from a misimplemented builder would otherwise flow into baseline math and
33	        # surface as an opaque downstream error. bool is excluded (it is an int subclass but not
34	        # a feature value). NaN/inf are rejected as not finite numeric features (#149).
35	        import math
36	
37	        for i, r in enumerate(self.rows):
38	            for v in r:
39	                if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, (int, float)):
40	                    raise TypeError(
41	                        f"FeatureMatrix.rows[{i}] contains a non-numeric value {v!r} "
42	                        f"({type(v).__name__}); features must be int/float"
43	                    )
44	                if not math.isfinite(v):
45	                    raise ValueError(f"FeatureMatrix.rows[{i}] contains a non-finite value {v!r}")
46	
47	
48	class FeatureBuilder(Protocol):
49	    """Build dataset-specific numeric features from a canonical corpus."""
50	
51	    def build(self, corpus: Corpus) -> FeatureMatrix:
52	        """Compute features for (a subset of) the corpus units. Must be deterministic."""
53	        ...
54	
55	    @property
56	    def provides(self) -> Sequence[str]:
57	        """Names of the features this builder produces (for provenance)."""
58	        ...
59

1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""

81	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
82	class GateReview:
83	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
84	
85	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
86	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
87	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
88	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
89	    """
90	
91	    gate: str
92	    frm: Phase
93	    to: Phase
94	    summary: str
95	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
96	
97	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
98	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
99	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
100	
101	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
102	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
103	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))

106	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]

108	
109	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
110	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137).
111	
112	    The source's self-reported ``claim_fingerprint()`` is an unverified self-report — it can say
113	    anything. The engine therefore computes its own type-faithful hash over the materialized
114	    records (in scored order) and records THAT as the authoritative fingerprint, so an auditor
115	    re-deriving the fingerprint from the recorded/scored claims gets the value the trail records.
116	    """
117	    return hash_value(
118	        [
119	            {
120	                "claim_id": c.claim_id,
121	                "subject": c.subject,
122	                "referents": list(c.referents),
123	                "assertion": c.assertion,
124	                "provenance": c.provenance,
125	            }
126	            for c in claim_records
127	        ]
128	    )
129	
130	
131	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
132	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
133	
134	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
135	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
136	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
137	    """
138	    seen: set[str] = set()
139	    dupes: list[str] = []
140	    for i in ids:
141	        if i in seen:
142	            dupes.append(i)
143	        seen.add(i)
144	    if dupes:
145	        raise FirewallViolation(
146	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
147	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
148	        )
149	
150	
151	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
152	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
153	
154	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
155	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
156	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
157	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
158	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
159	    """
160	    return GateDecision(
161	        approved=True,
162	        gate=review.gate,
163	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
164	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
165	    )

172	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
173	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
174	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
175	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the

193	                n
194	                for n, v in (

205	                n
206	                for n, v in (
207	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
208	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
209	                )
210	                if v is None
